body.title: Rename inertia → confidence throughout memory layer
body.result: Validated by CC: Phase 1+2 complete. confidence→certainty DB column, inertia→confidence computed property, FACTUAL base 0.25→0.65. 115 targeted tests pass. No stale .inertia refs in source.
body.intention: I intend that the memory layer uses 'confidence' everywhere instead of 'inertia' so that the concept is self-documenting for anyone reading code or querying the DB.
body.description:
Rename `inertia` → `confidence` throughout the Igor memory layer. `BASE_INERTIA` dict → `BASE_CONFIDENCE`; `Memory.inertia` property → `Memory.confidence`; all callsites in cortex.py, main.py, push_sources.py, system_prompt.py; rename `tools/table_inertia.py` → `tools/table_confidence.py`. Also bump `FACTUAL` base confidence from 0.25 → 0.65 (currently underweighted — factual memories should resist drift more than procedural ones).

Note: `confidence` is a computed `@property`, not stored in the DB — no migration needed.

**HIGH-inertia pre-approval:** Akien approved this rename explicitly in conversation 2026-06-10 ('confidence makes complete sense').

**Affected files:** devices/igor/memory/models.py, devices/igor/memory/cortex.py, devices/igor/main.py, devices/igor/cognition/push_sources.py, devices/igor/cognition/system_prompt.py, devices/igor/tools/table_inertia.py → table_confidence.py
**Design rules:** none apply — pure rename, no logic change
**Scope boundary:** IN — rename + FACTUAL baseline bump. OUT — adding LEVER type (T-lever-memory-type), source seeding (T-memory-source-seed).
**Completion criteria:** `grep -r '\.inertia\b\|BASE_INERTIA' devices/igor/ --include='*.py'` returns zero hits; all tests pass; `Memory.__repr__` shows `confidence=` not `inertia=`.

## Escalation summary
**What was tried:** Confirmed: the rename **breaks instantiation**. The dataclass auto-generated `__init__` tries to set `self.confidence = 1.0` (the G46 stored field default), but `confidence` is now a read-only property → `AttributeError`. Every `Memory(...)` construction fails.

This is a genuine collision the ticket did not anticipate: there is a pre-existing distinct G46 stored `confidence` field (epistemic certainty, 0–1, settable) that is semantically different from the renamed `inertia` property (network-po...
**Where it broke:** result missing DONE: prefix — not a completion
**What now?**

## CC Resolution Plan (2026-06-11)

Dick/opus correctly identified the blocker: stored field `confidence: float = 1.0` (G46 epistemic certainty) already exists, so renaming the `@property inertia` → `confidence` causes a Python dataclass collision.

**Two-phase approach:**
Phase 1 — rename stored field `confidence → certainty` everywhere + DB migration `ALTER TABLE memories RENAME COLUMN confidence TO certainty` (REAL DEFAULT 1.0, no data transform). Affects: cortex.py SQL blocks (1806, 1835, 1877, 1950, 1986), engram_export.py, versioning.py, coactivation_counter.py, turn_pipeline.py, consult.py.
Phase 2 — rename computed `@property inertia → @property confidence`, `BASE_INERTIA → BASE_CONFIDENCE`, all callsites: cortex.py (3659, 4305), main.py (8212, 8276, 8349), push_sources.py (250, 998), system_prompt.py (114), table_inertia.py → table_confidence.py, tests.
